concept: Set intersection of two small finite sets of integers
question: Let A = {1, 5, 9, 13} and B = {5, 7, 9, 11, 15}. What is A ∩ B?
solution: 1. List elements of A: 1, 5, 9, 13. 2. List elements of B: 5, 7, 9, 11, 15. 3. Check each element of A: 1 is not in B; 5 is in B; 9 is in B; 13 is not in B. 4. Therefore A ∩ B = {5, 9}.
paraphrase: First, note that A contains 1, 5, 9, and 13. Second, note that B contains 5, 7, 9, 11, and 15. Third, go through each member of A and ask whether it also appears in B: 1 does not, 5 does, 9 does, 13 does not. The elements common to both sets are 5 and 9, so the intersection is {5, 9}.
